title: Mannarasala Temple
tagline: Mannarasala Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Mannarasala Sree Nagaraja Temple (Malayalam: മണ്ണാറശാല ശ്രീ നാഗരാജാക്ഷേത്രം) in Haripad is a very ancient and internationally known centre of pilgrimage for the devotees of serpent
overview.description: Mannarasala Sree Nagaraja Temple (Malayalam: മണ്ണാറശാല ശ്രീ നാഗരാജാക്ഷേത്രം) in Haripad is a very ancient and internationally known centre of pilgrimage for the devotees of serpent Gods (Nagaraja). The famous Nagaraja temple "Mannarasala" in Haripad is nestled in a forest glade, like most snake temples. The Mannarasala Temple has over 100,000 images of snakes along the paths and among the trees, and is the largest such temple in Kerala, India. Couples seeking fertility come to worship here, and upon the birth of their child come to hold thanksgiving ceremonies here, often bringing new snake im

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kumarapuram, Alappuzha
topPlaces.description: Kumarapuram is a village in Alappuzha District of Kerala State of India. This place is mentioned in the famous Malayalam CBI (film series) and is the setting of Oru CBI Diary Kurippu.

topPlaces.name: Haripad
topPlaces.description: Haripad is a municipality in Onattukara region of Alappuzha District, Kerala, India located between Alappuzha and Kollam on the National Highway 66. Haripad is bordered on the east by Pallippad, Veeyapuram and Karuvatta to the north, Kumarapuram and Karthikappally to the West, and on the south by Cheppad.

topPlaces.name: Karthikappally
topPlaces.description: Karthikappally is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. Haripad is the headquarter of Karthikappally Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Valiyakulangara Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: Valiyakulangara Devi Temple is situated at Mahadevikadu village, in Karthikappally neighbourhood of Alappuzha district Nangyarkulangara in Kerala and is located about 4 km from Haripad and 3 km from Thrikkunnappuzha. It is located 3 km from the National Waterway-3 as well as from the National Highway-47.

topPlaces.name: Veeyapuram
topPlaces.description: Veeyapuram is a village in the district of Alappuzha, Kerala, India. It is located about 7 kilometers north of Haripad town.

topPlaces.name: Achankovil
topPlaces.description: Achankovil is a 128 km (80 mi) long west flowing river in Kerala, India. It flows through the districts of Kollam, Pathanamthitta and Alappuzha.

topPlaces.name: Cheruthana
topPlaces.description: Cheruthana is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Nangiarkulangara
topPlaces.description: Nangiarkulangara is a relatively small town situated near Haripad, Alappuzha district in the state of Kerala, India. The area postal pin code is 690513.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mannarasala Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mannarasala Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mannarasala Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (96 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (78 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mannarasala Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mannarasala Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mannarasala Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Mannarasala Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mannarasala Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kumarapuram, Alappuzha, Haripad, Karthikappally, Valiyakulangara Devi Temple, Veeyapuram.
